textPlain: Empowering individuals to solve collective problems rarely work.<https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=31178680>The appropriate solution is legislation.

This is precisely why I happily pay for an annual subscription to ConsumerLab[0]. It's largely just for supplements and a few functional foods, but with a tiny staff they are doing more work to help the public on the unregulated medicine market than the entire FDA, IMHO.[0]https://www.consumerlab.com/

The trend of making these medical tests cheaper and easier to obtain is going to result in a lot of positive change.  Certainly for individuals and hopefully the anonymized data helps get the spotlight on larger trends.
If you have ever visited the ruins of Pompeii, you might have seen all the lead pipes that provided water to the city. I wonder how that affected the health of the citizens back then.
I also recently subscribed to ConsumerLab, and I'm glad I did. I wish they could test products more frequently as things change from batch to batch, but it's a whole lot better than nothing.
My understanding is that the high calcium content in their water supply formed a lining on the inside of the pipes which largely prevented any exposure.
